records.normalized_title: Six-month stolen-bike recovery with Rob Porter
records.published_title_visible: 13. It took about 6 months to get Harry Leary’s stolen BMX back - they knew where it was - Rob Porter
records.published_title_evidence_status: transcribed from supplied YouTube Studio screenshot; an ellipsis marks visible UI truncation where present
records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - It took about 6 months to get the Harry Leary’s stolen loaner Daylight BMX back
records.working_source_description: It took about 6 months to get the Harry Leary’s stolen loaner Daylight BMX back. There were jurisdiction issues. Rob Porter helped because the guy who was selling the bike lived near him. After multiple purchase attempts, the bike was finally recovered after the thief finally agreed to meet. The bike was returned to Daylight BMX and one of Richard’s daughters is now riding it. It still has the magic mushroom decals on it.
records.frame_mapping_basis: chronological order of the 32 supplied published-frame captures, aligned to the Studio register that omits 10 and contains two distinct records numbered 12
records.transcript_status: No standalone Short transcript was supplied. The timestamp range is a provisional access reference into the supplied parent machine transcript and requires audiovisual verification.
records.medical_or_sensitive_claim_qualification: The theft, jurisdiction, recovery, and later use of the bicycle are interview recollections unless separately verified.
records.rights: User-supplied Lititz BMX publication evidence. No open license implied.
records.supplied_description: It took about 6 months to get the Harry Leary’s stolen loaner Daylight BMX back. There were jurisdiction issues. Rob Porter helped because the guy who was selling the bike lived near him. After multiple purchase attempts, the bike was finally recovered after the thief finally agreed to meet. The bike was returned to Daylight BMX and one of Richard’s daughters is now riding it. It still has the magic mushroom decals on it.

records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - Found Harry Leary’s Display Case at the National BMX HOF
records.working_source_description: Greg Mathias and Kyle A. Huffman found Harry Leary’s display case at the National BMX Hall of Fame. Inside the case, we notice a miniature HLT frame and fork that Harry Leary used to have displayed on his nightstand - it was one of his only possessions he kept at his apartment. He got it from someone in Australia.

records.normalized_title: Harry devalued personal gains
records.published_title_visible: 17. Harry Leary Devalued His Personal Gains - “I can’t imagine being in the mind of Harry Leary”
records.published_title_evidence_status: transcribed from supplied YouTube Studio screenshot; an ellipsis marks visible UI truncation where present
records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - Harry Leary devalued many of his personal gains
records.working_source_description: Harry Leary devalued many of his personal gains because. The riders were told to give the “schwag” away to promote the brand. He ended up keeping very little and holding very little to value. He had a locker in Lake Havasu that was sold off because he wasn’t paying for the unit. Greg never had Harry sign anything, even though he had given him things over the years - he thought he would be around forever.
records.frame_mapping_basis: chronological order of the 32 supplied published-frame captures, aligned to the Studio register that omits 10 and contains two distinct records numbered 12

records.supplied_description: Harry Leary devalued many of his personal gains because. The riders were told to give the “schwag” away to promote the brand. He ended up keeping very little and holding very little to value. He had a locker in Lake Havasu that was sold off because he wasn’t paying for the unit. Greg never had Harry sign anything, even though he had given him things over the years - he thought he would be around forever.

records.normalized_title: Locker, romance novels, and three of everything
records.published_title_visible: 18. The last thing Harry Leary cared about was his locker - we found romance novels - three of each
records.published_title_evidence_status: transcribed from supplied YouTube Studio screenshot; an ellipsis marks visible UI truncation where present
records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - The last thing Harry Leary Cared About At The Time Was His Locker
records.working_source_description: Smart people have three of everything! But… The last thing Harry cared about at the time was his locker. Greg and Kyle further talk about an episode Kyle had and Greg mentioned there was no one there to tell him otherwise. A while ago, Greg told Kyle to “chew your words.” This statement has helped Kyle out tremendously but Greg is now that little angel on Kyle’s left shoulder.

records.working_source_description: Greg Mathias donated a Harry Leary bobblehead to the National BMX Hall of Fame. The bobble head itself is wearing a Fall Risk Racing kit, which was Harry Leary’s last sponsor. The Harry Leary Bobblehead Donation Was Made by Greg Mathias to the National BMX Hall of Fame #bmx BMX Team USA's Greg Mathias shares his experience donating a special Harry Leary bobblehead to the National BMX HoF. The figure, dressed in a Fall Risk Racing (FRR) kit—Harry's last sponsor—is just one example of Greg’s commitment to preserving Harry's legacy.

records.working_source_description: Greg and Kyle find the donation Kyle made to the National BMX Hall of Fame - The 1986 Porsche Design Fallnational plaque as won by Harry Leary. records.normalized_title: Diamond Back from Australia donated then sold

records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - He Talked with gOrk about an old DB Harry had on loan
records.working_source_description: When Greg took Harry’s old 24” Daylight BMX, he was talking with Gork about an old Diamondback Harry had on loan to the museum. Harry got an HLT from someone in Australia. Harry eventually took the bike out of the museum and sold it. The museum wanted Harry to keep it there but - “what are you going to do?” - Greg Mathias

records.supplied_description: When Greg took Harry’s old 24” Daylight BMX, he was talking with Gork about an old Diamondback Harry had on loan to the museum. Harry got an HLT from someone in Australia. Harry eventually took the bike out of the museum and sold it. The museum wanted Harry to keep it there but - “what are you going to do?” - Greg Mathias

records.normalized_title: One of BMX's most influential people
records.published_title_visible: 24. Greg Mathias, Harry Leary was “one of the most influential people” - “racing 50 frickin years”
records.published_title_evidence_status: transcribed from supplied YouTube Studio screenshot; an ellipsis marks visible UI truncation where present
records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - Harry Leary was one of the most influential people in the sport of BMX.
records.working_source_description: In Greg Mathias’ opinion, Harry Leary was one of the most influential people in the sport of BMX. He raced for over 50 years. Greg tells a story of how when he and Harry Leary first started training, Harry would go to Greg’s business. Greg’s wife works there too and didn’t know who Harry was so she asked Harry how long he has been doing this - “Oh about 55 years.” - Also, Greg reflects on younger people not knowing who Harry was but eventually we came to the decision that Harry is still relevant and will remain relevant in the sport of BMX.

records.normalized_title: Helpful to everybody, even kids
records.published_title_visible: 26. Harry Leary “was always helpful to everybody” - “even kids” - “never rude or anything” - “he lived it”
records.published_title_evidence_status: transcribed from supplied YouTube Studio screenshot; an ellipsis marks visible UI truncation where present
records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - Harry Leary was always helpful to everyone
records.working_source_description: Harry Leary was always helpful to everyone, even kids. He was also an avid mentor and a woman once reached out to tell Kyle that Harry was her daughter’s best friend. Her daughter turned out to be about 8 years old - Kyle sent her the jersey Harry was wearing in the picture that was originally shared with him.

records.normalized_title: Demon side and CTE discussion
records.published_title_visible: 27. Greg Mathias shares - Harry Leary “had his demon side as well” - the family didn’t test for CTE
records.published_title_evidence_status: transcribed from supplied YouTube Studio screenshot; an ellipsis marks visible UI truncation where present
records.working_source_title: Fireside BMX Chat w/ Greg Mathias - Harry Leary did have his demons
records.working_source_description: While Harry Leary can be thought of doing many wonderful things, he did have his demons. Friends and family did not test Harry for CTE, they just didn’t think about it at the time. He crashed many times and “stuck his head into the ground a few times pretty hard” - some of his helmets were nearly split in half. Harry also had a lot of concussions as a kid, when he would ride without a helmet.
